assertions.title: LLM Product graph node and surface exist

steps.action: Open the root graph workspace at http://127.0.0.1:3100.
steps.expected: A visible 'LLM Product' node is present in the graph shell.
steps.observed: The default workspace rendered an 'LLM Product' graph button alongside the other atlas nodes.

steps.action: Click the LLM Product node from the default graph state.
steps.expected: The app opens the LLM Product detail surface.
steps.observed: The URL changed to #graph-llm-product and the focused detail surface rendered 'LLM Product' with the summary 'A prediction-market-native model product with BRAID reasoning built in and sold as a standalone API subscription'.

evidence.network: GET / -> 200 confirmed via curl; node selection used client-side hash navigation with no API failures observed.

steps.action: Inspect the LLM Product surface copy after opening #graph-llm-product.
steps.expected: The surface visibly includes RL / parameterization / quantizing, BRAID reasoning DAG, $29/mo API pricing, $BETTER holder discounts, and OpenRouter credits revenue.
steps.observed: Visible copy stated the model is tuned via 'reinforcement learning, parameterization, and quantizing', described 'BRAID reasoning DAG' as the reasoning core, and labeled the monetization as a '$29/mo' standalone API subscription with 'scaling discounts for larger $BETTER holders' plus 'OpenRouter credits' sold on top.

evidence.network: Static surface content only; no API/network failures observed while inspecting the LLM detail surface.

assertions.title: TRUTH-PERP graph node and surface exist

steps.action: Open the root graph workspace at http://127.0.0.1:3100.
steps.expected: A visible 'TRUTH-PERP & Flywheel' node is present in the graph shell.
steps.observed: The default workspace rendered a 'TRUTH-PERP & Flywheel' graph button in the atlas node list.

steps.action: Open the TRUTH-PERP & Flywheel node from the graph.
steps.expected: The app opens the TRUTH-PERP & Flywheel detail surface.
steps.observed: The URL changed to #graph-truth-perp-flywheel and the focused surface rendered 'TRUTH-PERP & Flywheel' with the summary 'Hyperliquid HIP-3 capital moat, a synthetic Nasdaq of Truth index, and a buy-and-burn arb loop'.

evidence.network: GET / -> 200 confirmed via curl; node selection used local hash navigation with no API failures observed.

steps.action: Inspect the TRUTH-PERP & Flywheel detail surface.
steps.expected: The surface visibly includes HIP-3 on Hyperliquid, the 500k HYPE / $11M barrier, and the Nasdaq of Truth framing.
steps.observed: Visible copy stated 'HIP-3 on Hyperliquid', described the capital barrier as '500k HYPE ($11M)', and framed TRUTH-PERP as the 'Nasdaq of Truth' synthetic index.

evidence.network: Static content only; no API/network failures observed while reviewing Hyperliquid and HIP-3 details.

steps.action: Review the vBETTER and flywheel sections of the TRUTH-PERP surface.
steps.expected: The surface visibly includes vBETTER arbitrage, 30% net arb profits to buy & burn, and the three-phase flywheel.
steps.observed: Visible copy described 'vBETTER' as the vault token used for 'ETF premium capture', stated '30% net arb profits → buy & burn', and enumerated the three flywheel stages as 'Phase 1 Vault — Q1 2026', 'Phase 2 Arbitrage — Q2 2026', and 'Phase 3 TRUTH-PERP — Q4 2026'.

evidence.network: Static content only; no API/network failures observed while inspecting the arbitrage flywheel sections.

summary: Tested 5 assertions for the LLM Product and TRUTH-PERP & Flywheel content-depth flow: 5 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked. Both graph nodes opened the intended detail surfaces and the required pricing, reasoning, Hyperliquid, vBETTER, buy-and-burn, and three-phase flywheel copy was visibly present without console errors.
